SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.hazid

Class HazopQuantificationLimits

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.hazid.HazopQuantificationLimits
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class HazopQuantificationLimits extends Object implements Serializable
Design limits used by HazopConsequenceAutoPopulator.quantify(neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem, neqsim.process.safety.hazid.HazopQuantificationLimits) to turn a computed consequence value into a deterministic PASS / EXCEEDS verdict.

Two screening limits are carried, each with a sensible default and an optional per-unit override keyed by unit name:

  • Maximum discharge temperature — the highest gas temperature a compressor or expander discharge may reach before seal, lube-oil or downstream material limits are challenged. Default 150 °C (a common API 617 screening value).
  • Minimum design metal temperature (MDMT) — the lowest metal temperature the material remains tough at, below which Joule-Thomson auto-refrigeration across a valve risks brittle fracture. Default -46 °C (a common low-temperature carbon-steel value per ASME UCS-66).

The defaults are conservative screening values only; a competent engineer should supply the actual equipment data sheet and material MDMT before relying on a verdict.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    HazopQuantificationLimits()
    Construct a limits holder with the conservative default screening limits.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    basisForMaxDischargeTemperature(String unitName)
    Produces an auditable basis string explaining the maximum discharge temperature limit applied to a unit, stating the value, whether it is a per-unit override or the screening default, and the governing standard.
    String
    basisForMinDesignMetalTemperature(String unitName)
    Produces an auditable basis string explaining the minimum design metal temperature (MDMT) limit applied to a unit, stating the value, whether it is a per-unit override or the screening default, and the governing standard.
    double
    maxDischargeTemperatureC(String unitName)
    Resolves the maximum discharge temperature limit for a unit, preferring a per-unit override over the default.
    double
    minDesignMetalTemperatureC(String unitName)
    Resolves the minimum design metal temperature limit for a unit, preferring a per-unit override over the default.
    HazopQuantificationLimits
    setMaxDischargeTemperatureC(double temperatureC)
    Sets the default maximum discharge temperature applied to compressors and expanders without a per-unit override.
    HazopQuantificationLimits
    setMaxDischargeTemperatureC(String unitName, double temperatureC)
    Adds a per-unit maximum discharge temperature override.
    HazopQuantificationLimits
    setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC(double temperatureC)
    Sets the default minimum design metal temperature applied to valves without a per-unit override.
    HazopQuantificationLimits
    setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC(String unitName, double temperatureC)
    Adds a per-unit minimum design metal temperature override.
    String
    toJson()
    Serialise the effective limits and any per-unit overrides to a pretty-printed JSON object so the policy used for a HAZOP screening run is auditable.

  • Method Details

    • setMaxDischargeTemperatureC

      public HazopQuantificationLimits setMaxDischargeTemperatureC(double temperatureC)
      Sets the default maximum discharge temperature applied to compressors and expanders without a per-unit override.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - maximum discharge temperature in degrees Celsius
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC

      public HazopQuantificationLimits setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC(double temperatureC)
      Sets the default minimum design metal temperature applied to valves without a per-unit override.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - minimum design metal temperature in degrees Celsius
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • setMaxDischargeTemperatureC

      public HazopQuantificationLimits setMaxDischargeTemperatureC(String unitName, double temperatureC)
      Adds a per-unit maximum discharge temperature override.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit operation name the override applies to
      temperatureC - maximum discharge temperature in degrees Celsius for that unit
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC

      public HazopQuantificationLimits setMinDesignMetalTemperatureC(String unitName, double temperatureC)

      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit operation name the override applies to
      temperatureC - minimum design metal temperature in degrees Celsius for that unit
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

      public double maxDischargeTemperatureC(String unitName)
      Resolves the maximum discharge temperature limit for a unit, preferring a per-unit override over the default.

      unitName - the unit operation name
      Returns:
      the applicable maximum discharge temperature in degrees Celsius

      public double minDesignMetalTemperatureC(String unitName)
      Resolves the minimum design metal temperature limit for a unit, preferring a per-unit override over the default.

      unitName - the unit operation name
      Returns:
      the applicable minimum design metal temperature in degrees Celsius

    • basisForMaxDischargeTemperature

      public String basisForMaxDischargeTemperature(String unitName)
      Produces an auditable basis string explaining the maximum discharge temperature limit applied to a unit, stating the value, whether it is a per-unit override or the screening default, and the governing standard.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit operation name
      Returns:
      a human-readable provenance string for the maximum discharge temperature limit

    • basisForMinDesignMetalTemperature

      public String basisForMinDesignMetalTemperature(String unitName)
      Produces an auditable basis string explaining the minimum design metal temperature (MDMT) limit applied to a unit, stating the value, whether it is a per-unit override or the screening default, and the governing standard.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit operation name
      Returns:
      a human-readable provenance string for the minimum design metal temperature limit

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serialise the effective limits and any per-unit overrides to a pretty-printed JSON object so the policy used for a HAZOP screening run is auditable.
